title: Rock Paper Scissors
description: Progress from a local rock-paper-scissors game to a full on-chain product with leaderboard and multiplayer. Each quest unlocks a new layer of the Polkadot stack.

quests.title: Local Challenger
quests.summary: Play vs. the computer. Results in local storage only. Mod the UI, tweak theming, or give the computer a personality. No contract changes required.
acceptance: App deployed via `dot deploy` and accessible on `<your-name>.dot` inside Polkadot Desktop
At least one custom modification (visual or behavioral)

quests.title: On-Chain Record
quests.summary: Save game results to Bulletin Chain. Keep the latest CID per account in localStorage (`rps-game-cid:<address>`) so the app can resolve the player's history on reload. In Level 3 this pointer moves on-chain.
acceptance: CIDs are generated and fetchable from the public IPFS gateway
Latest CID per account is tracked in localStorage under `rps-game-cid:<address>`
On reload, the app reads the stored CID and rehydrates the profile from Bulletin

quests.title: The Leaderboard
teaches: On-chain Mapping storage vs off-chain Bulletin storage
Public leaderboard pattern (contract = index, Bulletin = storage)
quests.summary: Deploy a smart contract for indexing. Build a leaderboard connected to account addresses. Scores are attributed to a player's on-chain identity.
acceptance: Contract deployed on Paseo Asset Hub
Points persist across clients and accounts
Any player can be inspected via the leaderboard UI

quests.title: Multiplayer
quests.summary: Challenge another player. P2P game via Statement Store. Create a game, share a challenge link (or QR code), opponent accepts, you play live with commit-reveal anti-cheat.
acceptance: Two different accounts can complete a full best-of-3 match
Neither player can see the opponent's move before committing their own
Both players' leaderboard entries update correctly
